messages.summary: URGENT FIX: The "Gateway Offline" notification appears on EVERY page even when gateway is running. F
messages.originalContent:
URGENT FIX: The "Gateway Offline" notification appears on EVERY page even when gateway is running. Fix it completely.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.

DESIGN PROTECTION: Do NOT modify sidebar.tsx, navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, or globals.css.

Task 1: Open src/components/notifications/notification-system.tsx (or wherever the Gateway Offline banner is). Find where it checks gateway status. It currently checks incorrectly. Fix the logic:
- Fetch /api/bridge/status
- If response has gateway.status === 'running' OR services array has a running gateway → gateway IS online → do NOT show warning
- If fetch fails OR gateway not running → show warning
- Add try/catch so if Bridge API is down, show "Connecting..." not "Gateway Offline"

Task 2: The notification "CPU usage has been above 85%" is fake/hardcoded. Remove it. Only show REAL notifications:
- Gateway status (from /api/bridge/status)
- Real CPU alert only if /api/bridge/metrics returns cpu > 85
- Build/deploy status

Task 3: The notification bell in the top bar shows a red badge with count. Make sure the count matches REAL unread notifications only. If no real alerts, badge should be hidden.

Task 4: Also check if there is a NotificationBell.tsx component that duplicates this logic. If yes, consolidate into one system.

After ALL: npm run build && pm2 restart genplatform-app && git add -A && git commit -m "Fix Gateway Offline notification + remove fake alerts" && git push

🔗 Preview: https://app.gen3.ai/dashboard
Do all 4 tasks NOW.

tasks.originalDescription: Open src/components/notifications/notification-system.tsx (or wherever the Gateway Offline banner is). Find where it checks gateway status. It currently checks incorrectly. Fix the logic:

microTasks.change: Add try-catch wrapper and fix gateway status check logic
microTasks.description: Locate the function or useEffect hook that fetches gateway status (likely named checkGatewayStatus, fetchGatewayStatus, or similar). Find the fetch call to /api/bridge/status. Wrap the entire fetch operation in a try-catch block. Inside the try block, parse the JSON response and check if response.gateway?.status === 'running' OR if response.services?.some(s => s.name === 'gateway' && s.status === 'running'). Store this boolean in a variable called isGatewayOnline. Only set the gateway offline warning state to true when isGatewayOnline is false.

microTasks.change: Show 'Connecting...' instead of 'Gateway Offline' when API unreachable
microTasks.description: In the catch block of the gateway status check function, instead of showing 'Gateway Offline' error, set a different state like setConnectionStatus('connecting') or setGatewayStatus('connecting'). This handles the case when the Bridge API itself is unreachable. Create a new state variable if needed: const [connectionStatus, setConnectionStatus] = useState<'connected' | 'connecting' | 'offline'>('connecting'). In the catch block, call setConnectionStatus('connecting') and do NOT add a gateway offline notification.

microTasks.change: Update banner render conditions to show correct status
microTasks.description: Find the JSX that renders the 'Gateway Offline' banner or notification. This is likely a conditional render like {showGatewayWarning && <Alert>Gateway Offline</Alert>}. Modify the condition to check: {connectionStatus === 'offline' && !isGatewayOnline && <Alert variant='destructive'>Gateway Offline</Alert>}. Add another conditional for connecting state: {connectionStatus === 'connecting' && <Alert variant='warning'>Connecting to Gateway...</Alert>}. Ensure the warning only appears when we've confirmed the gateway is actually offline, not just on initial load.

microTasks.change: Add 30-second polling interval for gateway status
microTasks.description: Add a polling interval to periodically check gateway status every 30 seconds. In the useEffect hook, add: const interval = setInterval(checkGatewayStatus, 30000). Return a cleanup function: return () => clearInterval(interval). This ensures the gateway status stays current. Also call checkGatewayStatus() immediately on mount. Make sure to clear any existing gateway offline notifications when the gateway comes back online by filtering the notifications array.

This ensures only real, unread notifications from API sources are counted. Store this in a useMemo for performance: const unreadCount = useMemo(() => notifications.filter(n => !n.read).length, [notifications]).

microTasks.change: Create settings API route file with directory helper
microTasks.description: Create a new API route file for settings. Import fs from 'fs/promises', path from 'path', and NextResponse from 'next/server'. Define const SETTINGS_FILE = '/root/genplatform/data/settings.json'. Create an async function ensureDataDir() that checks if /root/genplatform/data directory exists using fs.access(), and if not, creates it with fs.mkdir(path, {recursive: true}). This function should be called before any read/write operations to ensure the data directory exists.

microTasks.change: Add GET handler to read settings from JSON file
microTasks.description: In the same file, export an async function GET(request: Request). Inside, call await ensureDataDir(). Then try to read the settings file with fs.readFile(SETTINGS_FILE, 'utf-8'). Parse the JSON and return NextResponse.json(settings). In the catch block, if the file doesn't exist (error.code === 'ENOENT'), return a default settings object: {fullName: '', timezone: 'UTC', dailyReports: true, systemAlerts: true}. For other errors, return NextResponse.json({error: 'Failed to read settings'}, {status: 500}).

microTasks.change: Add PUT handler to write settings to JSON file
microTasks.description: In the same file, export an async function PUT(request: Request). Call await ensureDataDir(). Parse the request body with const body = await request.json(). Extract fullName, timezone, dailyReports, systemAlerts from body. Create a settings object with these values. Write to file using fs.writeFile(SETTINGS_FILE, JSON.stringify(settings, null, 2)). Return NextResponse.json({success: true, settings}). Wrap in try-catch, return status 500 on error with message 'Failed to save settings'.

microTasks.change: Add /api/settings to CSRF skip list in middleware
microTasks.description: Find the array or condition that lists paths to skip CSRF validation. This might be named csrfSkipPaths, publicPaths, or similar. Add '/api/settings' to this list. If the middleware uses a conditional check like path.startsWith(), add an OR condition: || path === '/api/settings' || path.startsWith('/api/settings'). This ensures both GET and PUT requests to /api/settings work without CSRF token requirements.

Import useState and useEffect from 'react'. Also import toast from your toast library (likely 'sonner' or 'react-hot-toast'). These states will track the form values and loading states.
